name: Modals
description: Overlay dialog for short tasks, confirmations, or focused content.

XS.useCase: Short confirmations with one or two lines of copy. Also usable as a small in-context pop-over without an overlay.

S.useCase: Brief multi-step dialogs, onboarding prompts, confirmation flows.

M.useCase: Default size for most form-based flows.

L.useCase: Data-heavy dialogs with tables, grids, previews, or complex actions like merge conflicts.

anatomy.name: Container
anatomy.description: Rounded surface holding all content; carries the drop shadow that separates it from the scrim. Padding and gap are 32px (24px at XS).

anatomy.name: Header
anatomy.description: Centered vertical title + subtitle stack, 4px gap, fills width.

anatomy.name: Title
anatomy.description: Primary heading, Open Sans Bold 24px / 38px line-height.

anatomy.name: Subtitle
anatomy.description: Optional descriptive text below the title, Open Sans Regular 12px / 16px. Toggled via showSubtitle (default visible).

anatomy.name: CloseIcon
anatomy.description: 24x24 X icon absolutely positioned in the top-right corner (inset by container padding). Always rendered; exits the entire flow in multi-step modals.

anatomy.name: BackIcon
anatomy.description: 24x24 CaretLeft icon absolutely positioned in the top-left corner. Visible only in multi-step modals.

anatomy.name: Content
anatomy.description: Slot where the calling context injects its body (forms, text, lists, tables, media). Placeholder spec: horizontal, fill width, 10px padding, 10px gap, centered, cornerRadius 16, height 196.

anatomy.name: Actions
anatomy.description: Horizontal button row at the bottom: fill width, hug height, 16px gap, right-aligned (primaryAlign MAX), vertically centered.

anatomy.name: SecondaryButton
anatomy.description: Cancel / secondary action, cornerRadius 8, Open Sans SemiBold 14px / 24px. Becomes 'Back' in multi-step modals.

anatomy.name: PrimaryButton
anatomy.description: Primary / confirm action, right-most, cornerRadius 8, Open Sans SemiBold 14px / 24px.

buttonPlacement.twoButtons: Secondary + primary together fill the right 50% of the footer (25% each).
buttonPlacement.oneButton: Primary sits at the right taking 25% of the footer width, or hugs its content.
buttonPlacement.threeButtons: Tertiary (text) button on the left, secondary + primary on the right, 25% width each.
buttonPlacement.leftHalf: The left half of the footer is reserved for optional info text (e.g. 'Models enabled: 3/12').
buttonPlacement.xsException: In XS modals buttons stretch to fill 100% of the row: 1 button → 100%, 2 → 50% each, 3 → 33% each.
buttonPlacement.order: Primary action right-most, secondary (cancel) to its left. Never put destructive actions on the right.

multiStep.behavior: When a modal contains multiple steps, replace Cancel with Back and add a CaretLeft back icon in the top-left corner. The X close icon stays in the top-right and exits the entire flow. Never stack a modal on another modal; replace the current modal's content instead.

do: Use a modal only for tasks that need full attention or must be completed before returning to the page.
Keep titles short and action-oriented: state what the user is about to do.
Primary action on the right, secondary (cancel) to its left, consistently across the product.
Choose the smallest size that fits the content comfortably; most modals should be Medium or smaller.
Always provide at least one explicit dismissal (X icon, Cancel button, or Escape).
dont: Don't use a modal for content that could live inline.
Don't stack a modal on top of another modal; replace the current modal's content for a second step.
Don't put destructive actions on the right; the right-most slot is for the safe, primary action.
Don't scroll inside Large modals when Medium plus an inline page would work; Large is for genuinely wide content like tables.
guidelines.sizing: XS for small in-context pop-overs that don't need an overlay. S or M depending on content size. L for complex actions such as solving merge conflicts.
considerations: Body contains a form → prefer Medium or Large so inputs aren't cramped and error text fits below fields.
Destructive action → use role='alertdialog' and phrase the primary button as the destructive verb ('Delete', not 'OK').
Vertical overflow → let the body scroll internally rather than making the modal taller than the viewport.
On open, move focus to the first interactive element; restore focus to the trigger on close.

aria: aria-describedby → subtitle/body when it adds context
keyboard: Tab/Shift+Tab cycle focus, trapped inside the modal
Enter triggers the primary action from non-button controls

notes: Pattern, not a base component (not in the component registry). Overlay/scrim color and opacity are not specified in the sources. Button height/padding are not specified in the Figma spec (the docs preview uses 6px 12px padding, illustrative only). L width is 1426px in the Figma spec; the docs page labels it '80% Screen Width'.
